behavior_summary: U8P_MUSHGRUP::look is a simple U8P_MUSHGRUP::look/inspect handler that displays the single bark text 'mushroom' to the player and then suspends the U8P_MUSHGRUP::look process.

U8P_MUSHGRUP::use is a utility process that (when executed) can spawn mushroom item instances near this object's location. It maintains a local spawn counter (local3) to limit repeated spawns, reads the object's coordinates, creates one or more items using FREE::createItemsAtLocation with a small random count, marks created items with a status bit (0x0080) via Item::orStatus, and calls Item::fall so the items settle into the world.

The spawn path is guarded by a hard limit (local3 < 10) to avoid unlimited creation; local3 is initialized and incremented as part of process control. Several details (exact numeric meanings and some local reuse) are inferred from low-level remnants.
notable_control_flow: U8P_MUSHGRUP::look: immediate linear flow — show bark 'mushroom' then suspend.

U8P_MUSHGRUP::use: initializes local3 (counter), then branches: if 'this' condition is true it increments local3; otherwise, if local3 < 0x0A it executes spawn sequence. The spawn sequence reads coordinates (Item::getX/Y/Z), calls FREE::createItemsAtLocation(…, urandom(0x04), itemType, X), checks the returned handle, sets status 0x0080 on the created item(s) and invokes Item::fall.
Spawn attempt is bounded by the counter check (local3 < 10) which acts as a guard to prevent indefinite spawning.
open_questions: What exact in-game event or player action invokes U8P_MUSHGRUP::use (the script shows the routine but not its trigger)?
What specific item type ID corresponds to the literal 0x01D1h used in FREE::createItemsAtLocation (metadata shows a literal but its game meaning is inferred)?
Does the status bit 0x0080 applied by Item::orStatus represent a known semantic (e.g., temporary flag, 'do-not-pickup', or 'just-spawned') in the broader codebase?
Is local3 reused as both the spawn counter and a Z coordinate in different branches intentional (appears in raw nodes) and could that cause subtle behavior depending on call order?

main_function.name: U8P_MUSHGRUP::look
main_function.purpose: Present a short description when the player inspects/examines this object (shows 'mushroom').
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player/Avatar performs the 'U8P_MUSHGRUP::look' or examine action on this item/NPC (standard U8P_MUSHGRUP::look handler).

function_map.name: U8P_MUSHGRUP::look
function_map.role: Player-facing description handler that barks 'mushroom' when examined.

function_map.name: U8P_MUSHGRUP::use
function_map.role: Utility/spawn routine that creates mushroom item instances near this object's location and marks/settles them; uses a local counter to limit spawns.

external_calls.purpose: Read the object's X coordinate for spawn positioning.
Read the object's Y coordinate for spawn positioning (captured but not shown used elsewhere in excerpt).
Read the object's Z/stack-height numeric field; appears to be stored into local3 in the spawn path (dual U8P_MUSHGRUP::use with the spawn counter).
Factory call to create one or more item instances at the computed location; receives type/category flags, urandom() count, item type id, and X coordinate; returns handle(s).
Provide a small random integer (range 0x04) to vary item creation count.
Set/OR a status bit (0x0080) on the created item handle(s) to mark them (semantic of bit inferred).
Cause created item(s) to settle/fall into the world (finalize placement/physics).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_MUSHGRUP::look is a simple U8P_MUSHGRUP::look/inspect handler that displays the single bark text 'mushroom' to the player and then suspends the U8P_MUSHGRUP::look process.
